metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'event attribution (denormalized author/author_email, author_id, replay back-compat)' to AC1's 'covers ALL of:' list so the criterion verifies this described deliverable.
findings.finding: The What section describes 'event attribution' (denormalized author/author_email + optional author_id; replay back-compat) as a substantive section of docs/identity.md, but AC1's explicit 'covers ALL of:' list does not include event attribution. An implementer could pass AC1 while omitting this section.
findings.location: What §1 (event attribution bullet) vs. AC1 checklist item
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, extend the list to: '…key-rotation/era-scoping, the provider-neutral seam, and event attribution (denormalized author/author_email + optional author_id, replay back-compat).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'authorship attestation (rebar.authorship.v1 / SSHSIG, trust root = in-band keys)' to AC1's 'covers ALL of:' list so this described deliverable has a verifying criterion.
findings.finding: The What section describes 'Authorship attestation (rebar.authorship.v1)' — SSHSIG over the foundation attest substrate — as a substantive section of docs/identity.md, but AC1's explicit 'covers ALL of:' list does not include authorship attestation. An implementer could pass AC1 while omitting this section.
findings.location: What §1 (authorship attestation bullet) vs. AC1 checklist item
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, extend the list to include: '…and authorship attestation (rebar.authorship.v1 SSHSIG, trust root = in-band keys).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'epoch-scoped key validity (merged-log-position, not wall-clock)' as a fourth required decision in AC line 2 of the ADR, citing the git-bug `ValidKeysAtTime` / HLC prior art from `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md §P2.2` as the context and justification.
findings.finding: The plan's ADR required-content list names three decisions (git-bug-entity, opaque-id-keying, in-band-TOFU) but omits the architecturally novel 'epoch = merged-log-position, not wall-clock' key-validity decision — the most security-relevant divergence from git-bug's Lamport-time model — leaving that decision undocumented in the ADR without a prior-art citation.
findings.location: ADR section (docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md) — required decisions list / AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: In AC line 2, extend the required decision list to: 'records the git-bug-entity, opaque-id-keying, in-band-TOFU, **and epoch-scoped key validity (merged-log-position not wall-clock, with prior art: git-bug ValidKeysAtTime, HLC event ordering)** decisions (context / decision / consequences)'. This ensures the completeness gate enforces the security-critical epoch-validity rationale.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/README.md` to `file_impact` (it must be edited to link `docs/identity.md` so the `check_docs_index.py --check` gate passes per AC3).
findings.finding: `docs/README.md` (the docs index) is an edit target required by What/Section 3 ('Add `docs/identity.md` … to the docs index so the docs-index completeness gate stays green') and AC3 (`check_docs_index.py --check` exits 0), but it is absent from `file_impact`, which lists only `docs/identity.md` and `docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md`.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `{"path": "docs/README.md", "reason": "add docs/identity.md link under the Operator section so check_docs_index completeness gate passes"}` to file_impact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the exact path of the docs index file in file_impact (e.g. `docs/index.md` or `docs/README.md`) so an executor can act without discovering it from the script source.
findings.finding: The docs-index file that must be edited is referred to only as 'the docs index file' in file_impact, without a concrete path; an executor must discover which file to modify before acting.
findings.location: ## file_impact — 'the docs index file (add the new docs so check_docs_index passes)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add the resolved path to file_impact: e.g. `docs/index.md` (add entries for `identity.md` and `adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Normalise 'era' vs 'epoch' to a single term throughout the plan (What §1, AC1) and carry that single term into both docs/identity.md and the ADR.
findings.finding: The plan uses 'era-scoping' in the What section heading and in AC1, but 'epoch-scoped validity' in the What section body description for the same concept, creating terminology drift that could cause confusion about whether these are the same mechanism.
findings.location: What §1 (key rotation bullet body) vs. What §1 heading 'Key rotation / era-scoping' vs. AC1 'key-rotation/era-scoping'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `identity.require_authenticated` (and its `IdentityConfig` section) was actually shipped in the stacked commits — if not, remove or qualify the enforcement section in docs/identity.md and the corresponding AC coverage requirement.
findings.finding: `identity.require_authenticated` does not exist in the codebase: there is no `IdentityConfig` dataclass and no `identity` section in `_SECTION_CLASSES` / `_SECTIONS` in `_config_schema.py`. The plan's `docs/identity.md` section on opt-in enforcement (AC line: 'covers opt-in enforcement + the merge-gate-is-the-control caveat') references a config key that is not present in the shipped code, so a doc written to describe `identity.require_authenticated` would document a non-existent feature.
findings.location: What / Section 1 – 'Opt-in enforcement: `identity.require_authenticated`, the write-gate' + AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Search the stacked commits (ddbe/4e9d/bde5/e165/264f/2f13/3183) for any `IdentityConfig` or `require_authenticated` addition; if absent, the plan's doc spec should describe only the enforcement model as a design goal / future gate, not as a currently-operative config key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `rebar verify-authorship` was shipped in the stacked commits; if not, either remove it from the doc spec or add a caveat that verification is done via the library API (`authorship.verify_authorship`) / add it as a deliverable gap.
findings.finding: `rebar verify-authorship` does not exist as a CLI command: it appears nowhere in the CLI dispatch table, the help directory, or the `reads_cli.py` subcommand registry. The plan's `docs/identity.md` spec explicitly names it as a tool operators and agents should use, but no such command is registered in the shipped codebase.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a lightweight grep-based or section-header proving command for AC1 and AC2 (e.g. `grep -qF '## Key rotation' docs/identity.md`) so topic coverage is machine-verifiable, not only implicitly manual.
findings.finding: AC1 and AC2 verify content completeness only through implicit manual review; there is no concrete, deterministic proving command that would confirm each named topic/decision is actually present in the produced files.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to each AC a sample verification such as: `grep -qF 'merge-gate' docs/identity.md && grep -qF 'Modify-Reporter' docs/identity.md && grep -qF 'era-scop' docs/identity.md` for AC1; `grep -qF 'opaque' docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md && grep -qF 'TOFU' docs/adr/0045-authenticated-identity.md` for AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to `docs/identity.md` the non-happy-path outcomes of `rebar verify-authorship`: what a revoked-key or epoch-mismatch result looks like, whether old events signed by rotated-out keys are accepted/rejected, and what remediation steps an operator should take.
findings.finding: The documentation spec does not require covering what happens when `rebar verify-authorship` encounters a revoked or missing key — i.e., how an operator interprets a verification failure on historical events after a key rotation, and what remediation steps exist. This is the most consequential operator-facing failure path for the key-rotation/era-scoping feature.
findings.location: docs/identity.md — Key rotation / era-scoping section; AC line: 'key-rotation/era-scoping'
findings.suggested_fix: In the key-rotation/era-scoping section, add a subsection 'Verification of historical events' that explains: (1) events signed within a key's valid epoch are accepted even after that key is revoked; (2) `rebar verify-authorship` exit codes / output for each case (valid, epoch-mismatch, key-not-found); (3) when an operator should escalate vs. treat as expected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `rebar verify-authorship` is a CLI command shipped by the dependent tickets (ddbe/4e9d/bde5/e165/264f/2f13/3183); if not, replace it in the What spec with the actual invocation (e.g. the Python API `authorship.verify_authorship(…)` or the identity subcommand path).
findings.finding: The plan cites `rebar verify-authorship` as a concrete CLI command that operators can run (What/Section 1, Opt-in enforcement bullet), but no such CLI command exists anywhere in the codebase — `cli-reference.md` lists all registered CLI commands and `verify-authorship` is absent; only the library function `authorship.verify_authorship` and an internal `_verify_keyop_signature` helper exist.
findings.location: What / Section 1, Opt-in enforcement bullet: '`rebar verify-authorship`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify what `rebar verify-authorship` refers to in the opt-in enforcement section: either (a) note it is a Python API only (`authorship.verify_authorship()`) and document the programmatic usage pattern, or (b) if a CLI subcommand is intended, add it to the implementation scope — it does not currently exist as a dispatched `rebar` subcommand.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `docs/identity.md` will document `rebar verify-authorship` as a CLI command operators and agents can run, but no such subcommand exists in the codebase — `verify_authorship` is an internal Python function in `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py`, not a dispatched CLI entry point. If the doc is written as specified, it will describe a phantom command, causing confusion for every operator or agent who follows the guide.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the bare `rebar verify-authorship` reference in the doc spec with the actual Python API path (`rebar.attest.authorship.verify_authorship(envelope, identity_id)` / `verify_authorship_at_epoch(envelope, identity_id, epoch)`) and document it as a programmatic verification call. If a CLI entry point for authorship verification is genuinely needed by operators, scope that as a separate implementation task and note it as 'future work' in the doc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `rebar verify-authorship` CLI gap before writing docs: either wire `verify_authorship()` in `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` as a `rebar verify-authorship <ticket-id>` CLI subcommand (and add it to `file_impact`), or revise the plan to document the correct existing command (`rebar verify-signature`) or the Python API surface instead.
findings.finding: `rebar verify-authorship` is named in the plan as a concrete operator-facing CLI command that docs/identity.md should document (under 'Opt-in enforcement'), but no such subcommand is registered in the CLI dispatcher — the internal `verify_authorship()` Python function in `src/rebar/attest/authorship.py` is not wired to any `rebar` CLI arm.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `rebar verify-authorship` in the plan's What section with the actual mechanism: either (a) add `verify-authorship` as a thin CLI arm (analogous to `verify-signature`) that calls `authorship.verify_authorship` and document that, or (b) note that authorship verification is exposed only via the Python API `rebar.attest.authorship.verify_authorship(envelope, identity_id)` and the merge-gate, and document accordingly.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
